figures.caption: Figure 1. Age-associated queuine depletion correlates with cellular senescence.

Figure 3. Queuine supplementation extends healthspan in D. melanogaster. (A) Schematic of experimental design assessing queuine-mediated healthspan improvement in D. melanogaster. Wild-type flies and flies supplemented with queuine (50 ng per food bottle) were evaluated for weight, exercise capacity, heat stress resistance, olfactory memory, and antioxidant capacity at 10, 20, and 30 days post-eclosion. (B-G) Longitudinal analysis of healthspan parameters in control and queuine-supplemented flies (n=10 cohorts): body weight (B), exercise performance (D), heat stress survival (E), olfactory memory retention (F), and systemic antioxidant activity (G).
Figure 8. Systemic queuine supplementation counteracts metabolic dysregulation in aging.
